commit.message:
Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

feat: auto-populate document templates from product data (P6 #36)

Adds "Generate for Product" flow to the Document Templates page.
Users select a product and the template is pre-filled from their
organisation, product, and stakeholder data.

Backend (document-templates service):
- generateTemplateForProduct() fetches product name, version,
  distributionModel from Neo4j and stakeholder roles from Postgres
- inferVersionFormat() auto-detects SemVer/date-based/CalVer from
  the product's version field
- mapDistributionModel() converts DB values to human-readable text
  (e.g. saas_hosted → "SaaS (centrally hosted)")
- buildRolesTable() maps stakeholder role_keys (security_contact,
  compliance_officer, etc.) into a populated Roles table with names
  and emails; unfilled roles get [REVIEW] markers
- Selects correct delivery model section (SaaS vs on-prem) based
  on distributionModel
- Replaces instructions block with shorter "Review before finalising"
  header for generated documents
- GET /api/document-templates/:id/generate?productId=&versionFormat=
  &securitySuffix= endpoint

Frontend (DocumentTemplatesPage):
- "Generate for Product" button alongside existing "Download Template"
- Modal with product selector dropdown, optional version format
  override field, security suffix field (defaults to -sec1)
- "Generate & Download" fetches pre-filled Markdown and triggers
  browser download
- "Download Template" restyled as ghost button for visual hierarchy

commit.message:
GregSweeper v0.8: 50 levels, checkpoints, solvability, flag mode, game persistence, ARIA, performance

Major update with 28 new features:

Gameplay:
- 50 levels across 7 tiers (Beginner → Immortal, up to 14x14 grids)
- Checkpoint system every 5 levels (replaces lives — death resets to last checkpoint)
- Guaranteed board solvability via constraint-based solver (50-attempt retry)
- Flat 2 random power-ups per win for all modes
- Flag-mode toggle for mobile (tap to reveal vs tap to flag)
- Game state auto-persistence (resume on reload)
- Progress bar with checkpoint markers
- Cells remaining counter
- Daily streak display

UX/Accessibility:
- First-launch onboarding overlay for new players
- ?mode=daily deep links for share cards
- Scrambled visual share card (cheat-proof)
- Styled daily leaderboard input (replaces prompt())
- Toast queue system (no overlapping notifications)
- Modal exit animations (slide-down)
- Encouragement lines on loss
- Default to Challenge mode for new players
- ARIA roles on all modals and game board
- 30 themes redistributed across 50 levels

Performance/Backend:
- Fog of War visibility: O(N) bounded checks (was O(N×R×C))
- localStorage error handling (QuotaExceededError)
- Stats cache to reduce localStorage reads
- Firebase rate limiting (30s cooldown) + score validation
- Theme CSS consolidation: 30+ CSS custom properties replace repeated overrides

commit.message:
fix(bets): allow bet placement on live API events without DB odds

The bet placement flow required odds from the DB 'odds' table, but live
events from external APIs (ESPN, OddsPapi, etc.) only have odds in-memory
on the API response — never persisted to the DB. This caused ODDS_NOT_FOUND
errors when placing bets on any live event.

Fix: snapshotOdds now falls back to client-provided odds (with sanity
checks) when no DB row exists. Also fix event ID types — live APIs return
string IDs, not integers.

commit.message:
feat: pure-Rust egui GUI + v2.0.0 (forge-gui) (#30)

* feat: GUI polish — validation, progress, diff filters, completion stage (pre-egui)

Staged improvements to the Tauri/React GUI before the egui migration:
- inline field validation across all stage forms
- live job progress component with cancel support
- diff filter chips and search in DiffStage
- completion stage artifact list with copy/open-folder buttons
- stepper active-stage highlight and step numbering

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: replace Tauri/React GUI with pure-Rust egui frontend (v2.0.0)

Introduces forge-gui — a self-contained egui/eframe 0.33 desktop application
replacing the previous Tauri + React GUI. No Node.js, npm, or WebView runtime
required at install time.

Key capabilities:
- 5-stage pipeline stepper: Inject → Verify → Diff → Build → Completion
- Full inject form: identity, SSH, network, system, packages, firewall (with
  policy dropdown), containers, swap (wired end-to-end to SwapConfig), proxy
- Inline field validation with red error hints on required fields
- Progress bar + cancel button (JoinHandle::abort) for all async operations
- Log panel: timestamps, error/warn coloring, error count badge, copy button
- Doctor panel: floating dependency status window with ok/warn/error dots
- Completion stage: artifact sizes, copy-path + open-folder buttons for each artifact
- eframe persistence: form state saved across sessions (password excluded via #[serde(skip)])
- Dynamic window title (ViewportCommand::Title) showing active stage + running state
- Diff stage: filter chips (All/Added/Removed/Modified) + search, context-aware empty states
- Build stage: distro selector cards, inspect panel with SHA-256 copy, post-build actions
- Status bar with auto-dismiss (8s for success, permanent for errors)
- Sidebar: stage navigation with amber highlight on running stage, version display, doctor button

CI: C3 container updated from Node.js/Tauri to Rust/GTK3 forge-gui build test
Release: forge-gui binary optionally staged alongside forgeiso in packaging scripts
Version: workspace bumped to 2.0.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: xorriso diff engine — two-method fallback for file listing across versions

The original xorriso -exec stat_lstat . invocation failed on xorriso 1.5.6+
which rejects . and {} path tokens after the exec action.

New strategy:
  1. Try -exec lsdl (no path arg) — parses permissions + size from lsdl output
  2. Fall back to -find / -type f (paths only, size=0) as minimum viable diff

Adds parse_lsdl_output() which handles the lsdl format:
  perms nlinks uid gid size month day time/year 'path'

Also: Tauri GUI form components and stage forms updated for validation
consistency with the egui frontend.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(engine): make late_commands distro-aware — skip apt/ufw for Fedora/Arch

build_feature_late_commands() shared Ubuntu-specific commands (APT proxy
config, apt-get repos, Docker via apt, UFW) with the Kickstart %post
and Arch post-install paths. These commands would emit and fail silently
on non-Ubuntu systems.

Guard Ubuntu-specific sections behind is_ubuntu check (true unless distro
is Fedora or Arch):
- APT proxy config (apt.conf.d/99proxy) — Ubuntu only
- APT repos section (add-apt-repository, sources.list.d) — Ubuntu only
- Docker installation via apt — Ubuntu only
- UFW firewall — Ubuntu only; Fedora uses firewalld via %packages

/etc/environment proxy settings remain for all distros (distro-agnostic).
systemctl enable/disable, sysctl, swap, user groups all remain (generic).

Add test: late_commands_omit_apt_and_ufw_for_fedora (43 tests total).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(gui): eliminate horizontal scroll blowout — replace f32::INFINITY widths

Root cause: 36 grid cells used desired_width(f32::INFINITY), which makes
egui Grid columns request infinite width. Inside a vertical ScrollArea,
this creates a frame-to-frame expansion loop: column requests infinity →
content region grows → next frame's available_width() returns the grown
value → repeat. The persisted egui state cached the blown-up rect
(observed at 17,353px wide), making the bug permanent across sessions.

Fix (two parts):
1. Set both set_min_width and set_max_width on the scroll area content
   to the panel viewport width, capping the content region
2. Replace all 36 desired_width(f32::INFINITY) with
   desired_width(ui.available_width()), which returns the cell's actual
   available width from the previous frame's grid layout

The fix converges in 1-2 frames with proper widths. Users must delete
~/.local/share/forgeiso/app.ron once to clear the cached blown-up rect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(gui): eliminate runaway horizontal scrollbars in egui Grid cells

egui Grid cells report unconstrained available_width() during layout,
causing desired_width(ui.available_width()) to request infinite width
and push every grid wider than the window viewport.

Fix: compute col_w = (available_width - spacing * (cols-1)) / cols
before each Grid and use it as the explicit desired_width in all cells.

* fix: patch grub.cfg for both /casper/vmlinuz and /boot/vmlinuz paths

Ubuntu 22.04+ live and server ISOs use /casper/vmlinuz in grub.cfg, not
/boot/vmlinuz. The previous single-pattern replace silently did nothing
for all modern Ubuntu ISOs, meaning the autoinstall kernel parameter was
never injected. Now both patterns are tried; only the one present in the
grub.cfg will match.

Also fix wallpaper dconf command: used .to_string() on a literal containing
{} instead of format!(), so the file extension was never substituted.
Two regression tests added for the grub path fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: verify and diff failures after inject

Verify failures:
- verify() now degrades gracefully instead of hard-failing when no
  SUMS URL can be resolved or when the filename isn't found in the
  checksums file (e.g. renamed/injected ISOs). Returns computed SHA-256
  with matched=false and a descriptive expected message rather than Err.
- InjectOk handler now auto-fills verify.source with the original source

  match Ubuntu's official SHA256SUMS since filename and content differ.
- Verify result UI handles non-hex expected values (shows full text

- Updated UI description to clarify the verify step's purpose.

Diff failures:

  inject.source string, which may be a URL xorriso cannot open.
- InjectOk handler now sets diff.base = source_iso (local path) instead
  of inject.source (raw user input, potentially a URL).

Engine:
- BuildResult gains source_iso: PathBuf, populated by both build() and
  inject_autoinstall() from resolved.source_path.

fix(observability): set emf.user.id on span tags for user activity queries (#484)

Three issues preventing user activity page from finding results:

1. Gateway: ObservabilityContextFilter (order -90) read X-User-Id from
   request headers, but HeaderTransformationFilter (order 50) hadn't set
   them yet. Now reads user from the JWT principal via exchange attributes.

2. Worker: MdcEnrichmentFilter sets span attributes outside the
   DispatcherServlet scope (on the propagated parent span, not the
   worker's server span). Extended SpanIdCaptureInterceptor to also set
   emf.user.id, emf.user.email, and tenant attributes on the correct
   server span from within the DispatcherServlet scope.

3. Frontend: UserActivityPage sent the EMF internal UUID as userId but
   the span tag contains the email from the JWT sub claim. Changed
   dropdown to use user.email as the filter value. Also fixed tag
   references to use tagMap and OTEL v1.21+ semantic conventions.
